mechanism_narrative: Higher FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and tightens financing conditions, so an elevated federal funds rate level predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher FEDFUNDS levels do not have a negative 20-day return coefficient in walk-forward tests, or if triggered negative signals produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening labor income and higher recession risk, reducing expected corporate revenues and risk appetite; when unemployment deterioration crosses a material threshold, S&P 500 returns are negative over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months or crosses above 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if instances where UNRATE crosses the trigger have average 60-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional average, or if the downside hit rate is below 50% over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in RSAFS indicates resilient consumer spending and near-term revenue support for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when retail sales momentum is positive.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises for 3 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month positive RSAFS momentum is followed by below-median 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered cases, or if the signal coefficient is nonpositive in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum rises while FEDFUNDS also rises, equities face simultaneous margin pressure and discount-rate pressure, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change crosses above 3% and FEDFUNDS 3-month change exceeds 0.25 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS trigger has no incremental negative effect versus either proxy alone, or if average 20-day returns after the joint trigger are positive over two consecutive walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and future earnings pressure for cyclical sectors, so a negative industrial production momentum crossover predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO 3-month crossovers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the 60-day return spread versus positive INDPRO momentum is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above a high activity threshold indicate strong household formation, construction demand, and durable-goods spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST readings above 2000 are not associated with above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the positive return hit rate after the threshold falls below 50% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Positive PAYEMS momentum combined with UNRATE at or below 5.0 signals expanding labor income without visible unemployment stress, supporting consumption expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE is at or below 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS and UNRATE condition does not outperform the unconditional 5-day return average, or if the interaction term adds no positive explanatory power beyond the two single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 momentum captures broad real demand growth and supports aggregate earnings expectations, so stronger real GDP growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth has a nonpositive 60-day return coefficient in walk-forward regressions, or if positive GDP-growth periods underperform negative GDP-growth periods over rolling 5-year samples.
